tldr: Move from Anteon's hosted/self-hosted K8s observability to Microsoft Retina, an open-source eBPF network observability tool for Kubernetes.
why_switch: Retina is fully free and MIT-licensed (no usage-based billing).
Microsoft-backed project with active development and frequent releases.
eBPF-based network flow visibility without sidecars.
Cloud-agnostic: works on AKS, EKS, GKE, on-prem clusters.
what_to_export: List of monitored Kubernetes clusters and namespaces
User accounts, teams, and RBAC mappings
Historical metric/log data you want to preserve
export_method: Anteon does not advertise a single-click export. Use its API or self-hosted database to dump alert rules, dashboards, and recent metric/log data. For historical telemetry, query through the standard interfaces and write results to local Parquet/JSON files. Consult Anteon's official documentation for exact endpoints and export formats.
import_method: Retina installs into Kubernetes via Helm or its published manifests (see retina.sh docs). After install, configure capture and metric profiles, then point a Prometheus/Grafana stack at Retina's metrics endpoint. Recreate dashboards in Grafana and rebuild alert rules in Prometheus Alertmanager or your existing alerting layer. Retina focuses on network observability, so logging/tracing concerns may need separate tools.

concept_mapping.note: Both run as cluster-wide eBPF agents.
Retina exposes Prometheus-compatible metrics; you bring your own Grafana.
Rebuild thresholds against Retina metric names.
Retina is network-focused; pair with Loki/ELK for logs.
Use Tempo/Jaeger or another tracing backend alongside Retina.
No per-GB ingest fees; ops burden shifts to your team.

gotchas: Retina covers network observability, not full APM/logs/traces — plan a complementary stack.
eBPF requires a compatible kernel; verify on each node pool.
Metric names and labels differ from Anteon — dashboards must be rebuilt, not copy-pasted.
No managed offering: you own upgrades, storage sizing, and HA.
Historical Anteon data won't import cleanly; expect a fresh baseline.

email.subject: Migrating from Anteon to Microsoft Retina
email.body:
Hi team,

We're planning to move our Kubernetes observability from Anteon to Microsoft Retina. Retina is open-source, MIT-licensed, eBPF-based, and removes the data-ingestion billing risk we've seen with Anteon.

Scope of work: export current alert rules, dashboards, and integration configs from Anteon; install Retina via Helm; pair with Prometheus + Grafana; rebuild dashboards and alerts; layer in Loki/Tempo for logs and traces if needed.

Estimated effort: 8-24 hours, plus a parallel-run window before fully decommissioning Anteon.

Will share a tracking doc and run a checkpoint after the pilot cluster is wired up.

Thanks.
